description: Strict rules to prevent memory leaks and ensure efficient resource management by correctly handling component lifecycles and side effects.
philosophy: What is created must be destroyed. Every side effect must have a corresponding cleanup function to prevent silent performance degradation.

rules.name: Mandatory `useEffect` Cleanup
rules.description: Any `useEffect` hook that initiates a subscription, a timer, a WebSocket connection, or attaches a global event listener MUST return a cleanup function.

guidelines.correctExample: useEffect(() => { window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll); return () => window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleScroll); }, []);
useEffect(() => { const timerId = setInterval(doSomething, 1000); return () => clearInterval(timerId); }, []);
useEffect(() => { const unsubscribe = subscribeToData(id); return () => unsubscribe(); }, [id]);

rules.consequence: Failure to provide a cleanup function for persistent side effects is a guaranteed memory leak.

rules.name: State Updates on Unmounted Components
rules.description: State updates MUST NOT be called on a component that has already been unmounted.
rules.problem: This typically happens when an async operation (like a data fetch) completes after the user has navigated away from the page, causing a React warning and potential memory leaks.

solutions.description: A ref can track if the component is still mounted before calling a state setter.
solutions.codeExample: const isMounted = useRef(true); useEffect(() => { return () => { isMounted.current = false; }; }, []); if (isMounted.current) { setState(...); }

solutions.description: The `AbortController` API can be used to cancel fetch requests in the cleanup function of a `useEffect`.
solutions.codeExample: useEffect(() => { const controller = new AbortController(); fetchData({ signal: controller.signal }); return () => controller.abort(); }, []);

rules.note: Modern data fetching libraries like TanStack React Query handle this automatically, which is why they are preferred.

rules.name: Avoid Anonymous Functions in Props
rules.description: Do not pass anonymous functions (e.g., `() => doSomething()`) directly as props to memoized child components.
rules.problem: An anonymous function is recreated on every render, creating a new reference. This breaks memoization (`React.memo`) and forces the child component to re-render unnecessarily, consuming CPU and memory.
rules.solution: Wrap all functions passed as props in the `useCallback` hook to preserve their reference between renders.

implementationProtocol.title: Protocol for AI Code Generation (Memory Safety)

steps.name: Audit `useEffect` Hooks
steps.action: For every `useEffect` generated, analyze if it creates a persistent side effect. If yes, a cleanup function is mandatory.

steps.name: Secure Asynchronous State Updates
steps.action: When generating manual fetch logic inside a `useEffect`, implement a mechanism (like AbortController) to prevent state updates on unmounted components.

steps.name: Memoize Prop Functions
steps.action: Automatically wrap functions passed to child components in `useCallback` to optimize rendering performance and prevent unnecessary memory allocation.
